name: The Three Taifas Trail
name_original: Comarca de Jiloca – Bágena, Villanueva y Luco de Jiloca
description: This easy 16.7-mile touring cycling route in Comarca de Jiloca takes you through charming villages and along the Jiloca River.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Báguena traverse a landscape characterized by rolling terrain and historical pathways. The region features converted railway lines, known as Vías Verdes, offering relatively flat and accessible sections. Natural areas, such as the Laguna de Gallocanta, provide opportunities for scenic rides around wetlands. Elevations are generally moderate, with routes navigating through varied countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Báguena The most popular touring cycling route is Aguallueve de Anento and Vía Verde Loop from Daroca, a 17.7 miles (28.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a Vía Verde, offering a smooth surface for cycling. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail, a moderate 19.2 miles (30.9 km) path. This route circles the Laguna de Gallocanta, providing views of the wetland environment. Local touring cyclists also love the The Three Taifas Trail, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Touring cycling around Báguena is defined by Vías Verdes, wetland areas like Laguna de Gallocanta, and historical trails. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Báguena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Báguena?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Báguena, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy Vías Verdes to more challenging trails with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Báguena offers several easy touring cycling routes. The region's Vías Verdes, converted railway lines, provide relatively flat and accessible sections perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956698744/the-three-taifas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Three Taifas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 26.8 km long and generally takes about 1 hour 33 minutes to complete.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of touring cycling routes in Báguena?
A: Touring cycling routes around Báguena vary in length, with many falling between 25 km and 35 km. Durations typically range from 1.5 to 3 hours, depending on the route's distance and your cycling pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993369169/aguallueve-de-anento-and-via-verde-loop-from-daroca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento and Vía Verde Loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is 28.5 km and takes around 2 hours 2 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Báguena area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Báguena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956708468/laguna-de-gallocanta-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Gallocanta Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which circles the scenic wetland area.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The touring cycling routes around Báguena offer views of diverse natural and historical landmarks. You can encounter wetlands like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, and discover waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5297831" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguilay Waterfall and Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5182236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge of Luco de Jiloca&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through historical towns or villages?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through or near historical settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Estación de Daroca&lt;/a&gt; offers a chance to explore the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca&lt;/a&gt; and its impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Báguena?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Báguena is generally during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and pleasant. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn offers comfortable temperatures and often vibrant colors. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, while winters are cooler but still offer options for cycling.

Q: Are there any facilities or recreation areas along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, you can find facilities along some routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguilay Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; is a spot where you might find amenities or a place to rest during your ride.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I spot while cycling around Báguena?
A: The wetland areas, particularly around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallocanta Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, are renowned for birdwatching. You might spot various migratory birds, including cranes, especially during migration seasons. The general countryside also hosts typical Iberian wildlife.

Q: Are there any less crowded touring cycling routes in Báguena?
A: While popular routes like the Laguna de Gallocanta Loop can attract visitors, many of the region's Vías Verdes and historical pathways offer a more tranquil cycling experience, especially outside peak tourist seasons. Exploring some of the lesser-known local loops can also lead to quieter rides.

Q: What should I wear for touring cycling in Báguena during spring?
A: In spring, Báguena's weather can be variable. It's advisable to wear layers that you can easily add or remove. A moisture-wicking base layer, a light cycling jersey, and a windproof or waterproof jacket are good choices. Don't forget cycling shorts, gloves, and sunglasses. Temperatures can still be cool in the mornings and evenings.

Q: Are there options for more advanced touring cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: While many routes feature moderate elevations, the region also offers options for more advanced cyclists. Some routes include longer distances and more significant climbs, providing a greater challenge. There are 7 difficult routes available in the area for those looking for a more strenuous ride.
tips: This natural path oriented to cycle tourism is still under construction in some sections. It plans to connect Santander with Sagunto using the route of the old railway line that was planned to unite these two port cities, but was never completed.

Beautiful and quiet town in the interior of Teruel that is located on the banks of the Jiloca River. Within its heritage is the picturesque Mudejar-style church and its iconic medieval bridge with three arches that crosses the river.

In this charming town, the Mudejar tower of its parish church stands out above all. You can also find numerous palatial houses (one of them, Casa Lucías, with the longest façade in Teruel, in danger of demolition), the Gothic-Baroque convent of San Valentín and, crowning the town, its 14th-century Gothic-civil castle and Templar origins.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Comarca de Jiloca – Bágena, Villanueva and Luco de Jiloca touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route typically takes approximately 1 hour and 33 minutes to complete. It covers a distance of 26.8 kilometers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is categorized as an easy bike ride, suitable for all fitness and skill levels. Its low difficulty makes it an excellent option for families or those seeking a relaxed cycling tour.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Comarca de Jiloca – Bágena, Villanueva and Luco de Jiloca route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved, ensuring a smooth and comfortable ride for various types of touring bicycles. While some sections of the larger Santander-Mediterranean Nature Trail in the region might be unpaved, this specific segment is highlighted as being mostly paved and easy.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll encounter the Santander–Mediterranean Nature Trail – Villanueva del Jiloca, pass through the settlement of Luco de Jiloca, and near Báguena, which features a religious building.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails, including Las Tres Taifas, Río Jiloca y Camino de la Azucarera, Camino del Cid (Senderista), and GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT. It also utilizes sections of the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo (Campo de Daroca - Jiloca).

faqs.question: Is the Comarca de Jiloca – Bágena, Villanueva and Luco de Jiloca route suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Given its easy difficulty and predominantly paved surfaces, this route is an excellent choice for families and cyclists of all ages looking for a relaxed and enjoyable outing.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Comarca del Jiloca region offers varied landscapes, making it enjoyable across many seasons. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be warm, while winter cycling is possible but may require checking local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on public cycling routes in Spain, especially on shared-use paths like this one. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash and ensure they do not disturb other users or local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to use this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Comarca de Jiloca – Bágena, Villanueva and Luco de Jiloca route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking to access the cycling route, particularly near Villanueva del Jiloca?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as the route passes through villages like Villanueva del Jiloca, Bágena, and Luco de Jiloca, you can typically find public parking within or on the outskirts of these settlements to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through several villages such as Bágena, Villanueva del Jiloca, and Luco de Jiloca, you will likely find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms within these settlements. It's advisable to carry water and snacks, especially between villages.

waypoints.name: Santander–Mediterranean Nature Trail – Villanueva del Jiloca
